note: Sloping pointed majuscule | Contrast between thick and thin strokes; presence of serifs at the ends of thin strokes; the vertical strokes of rho, ypsilon, and phi end with a curve to the left; beta is maximized and has round curves; the oblique strokes of kappa are detached from the vertical stroke; the central strokes of mu are merged into a curve; omega has rounded curves; angle of inclination ca. 118°

bib.citation: Jaeger, Werner, ed. Two rediscovered works of ancient Christian literature: Gregory of Nyssa and Macarius. Leiden, 1954
note: About 52 lines, corresponding to three sides of text (one folio + one side), of the Jaeger edition are missing between 271.16 (fol. 176v) and 274.2 (fol. 182r). About 39 lines, corresponding to one folio, of the Jaeger edition are missing between 281.13 (fol. 171r) and 283.6 (fol. 177r); these correspond to two sides (one folio) of text.

bib.citation: Hjelt, Arthur, ed. Syrus sinaiticus. Helsingfors, 1930
Voicu, Sever J. Frammenti di un palinsesto greco di Efrem (Sinaiticus Syr. 30, f. 171ff.). Scriptorium 38 (1984)

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
